evals.prompt: The settings page in this app is pretty plain. Show me a few different ways the 'Data' section (import / export / clear all) could look — I want to pick one before you change anything.
evals.expected_output: A single self-contained mock.html with 3-4 distinct variants of the Data section, rendered in the app's real design tokens, light+dark aware, each with a short tradeoff note. Opens it and asks the user to choose.

evals.prompt: I don't love how the 'due for review' list items look on the home dashboard. Mock up a couple of options for the row design (name, ECO code, status badge, review button).
evals.expected_output: mock.html with 2-3 variants of the due-list row, using real tokens, both themes, framed in context. User is asked to pick; no live component edited before the pick.

evals.prompt: We need an empty state for the new Repertoire page for users who haven't added any openings yet. Give me some design directions to choose from.
evals.expected_output: mock.html with 3-4 genuinely different empty-state approaches (e.g. minimal CTA, guided steps, hero, quick-pick), real tokens, light/dark, tradeoff notes, opened, user asked to choose.
